executive_summary: The implementation satisfies all seven functional requirements and all three user stories from PRD-001. registry.ts is the single source of truth for per-model defaults, create.ts removes static commander defaults for dimension/sampling params and applies model defaults at runtime, and the --help footer for each media type renders a per-model defaults table generated dynamically from registry data. Four minor observations were identified: a duplicated bpm static default, unreachable hardcode fallbacks in handlers, a non-overridable fps parameter, and an untestable visual-verification criterion.

verification_by_fr.notes: registry.ts exports both the ModelDefaults interface (line 6) and getModelDefaults(media, model): ModelDefaults | undefined (line 34). create.ts imports and uses both.
All fields in ModelDefaults (width?, height?, length?, fps?, steps?, cfg?, bpm?) carry the optional marker. z_image correctly omits cfg; ltx23 correctly omits steps.

Commander .option() declarations for --width, --height, --length, --steps, --cfg carry no static defaults. --bpm retains '120' as a static default (create.ts:157), however bpm is not in AC01's explicit list of params to clear. The intent of FR-3 (commander resolution is undefined so handler detects omission) is fully satisfied for all listed params.
All three action handlers (image, video, audio) call getModelDefaults after validateModel and resolve each undefined param before constructing the *Opts object passed to buildArgs.

buildDefaultsTable(media) in create.ts generates the table by iterating getModels('create', media), filtering by getModelDefaults !== undefined, and reading values via getModelDefaults calls. No hardcoded strings.
VIDEO_MODEL_CONFIG entries (cfgFlag, omitSteps, t2v, i2v) are unchanged. buildVideoArgs still reads omitSteps and cfgFlag to decide --steps inclusion and flag name.
edit image and edit video handlers call notImplemented immediately; no default-value logic was added or touched in those branches.

verification_by_us.notes: AC01: ModelDefaults interface present with all required optional fields plus bpm (needed for ace_step). AC02: All models with create scripts have entries (image: sdxl/anima/z_image; video: ltx2/ltx23/wan21/wan22; audio: ace_step). AC03: getModelDefaults exported and callable. AC04: No type errors visible; import chain is clean.

AC01: No static defaults in commander options for listed params. AC02: getModelDefaults called in every handler. AC03: ltx2 → width=1280, height=720, length=97, steps=20, cfg-pass1=4 ✓. AC04: wan21 → width=832, height=480, length=33, steps=30, cfg=6 ✓. AC05: ace_step → duration=120, steps=8, cfg=1 ✓ (buildAudioArgs remaps length→--duration). AC06: ?? operator ensures user-supplied values are never overwritten. AC07: Types align across registry/create/builders.

AC01: addHelpText('after', buildDefaultsTable('video')) present; table columns are width/height/length/fps/steps/cfg for all four video models. AC02: create image --help table covers sdxl, anima, z_image (flux_klein and qwen correctly excluded by the getModelDefaults filter). AC03: create audio --help table uses DEFAULTS_COLUMNS audio mapping length→'duration'. AC04: All values sourced from MODEL_DEFAULTS via getModelDefaults. AC05: Static analysis confirms output structure; runtime visual verification not performed.

minor_observations: --bpm in create audio (create.ts:157) retains a static commander default of '120', duplicating the registry value. This is benign today but violates the 'registry as single source of truth' principle. Recommend removing the static default and relying solely on the ModelDefaults entry.

The video and image handlers carry hardcoded last-resort fallback strings inside the ?? chain (e.g., '832', '480', '20'). These are unreachable for all currently registered models, but silently hide missing registry entries for future models. Recommend removing them or replacing with a runtime error.

fps is displayed in the create video --help defaults table but there is no --fps CLI option, so users cannot override it. This is likely intentional (fps is a fixed model parameter), but the help text gives no indication of this. Consider adding a brief note or dropping fps from the display columns.

ltx23 shows '—' for steps in the video defaults table because it has omitSteps:true and no steps registry entry. This is correct, but first-time users may be confused. A footnote such as '(—) = param not used by this model' would improve clarity.
conclusions_and_recommendations: The implementation is production-ready for PRD-001 scope. All FRs and USs comply. The four minor observations are low-risk: the bpm duplication and hardcoded fallbacks are maintenance hazards worth addressing in a follow-up, while the fps table entry and ltx23 steps display are UX polish items. Recommended refactor actions: (1) remove the static '120' default from --bpm and let the handler resolve it from ModelDefaults; (2) remove unreachable fallback strings from the ?? chains in all three handlers; (3) optionally add a table footnote explaining '—' values.
